[tool result] worktrees

Here's the state of this worktree:

**Branch divergence** — `docker/multi-arch-prebuild` is 5 ahead / 6 behind `easel/docker/multi-arch-prebuild`. The commit *messages* match one-for-one but the SHAs differ, so this looks like a local rebase that hasn't been force-pushed. Needs reconciling before push.

**Uncommitted work in submodule** `dflash/deps/llama.cpp`:
- Submodule HEAD is detached at `ce3919b` (newer than the parent's recorded `dad4f26`)
- 82 added lines in `ggml/src/ggml-cuda/fattn.cu` are uncommitted inside the submodule
- This is real work that would be lost if the submodule is reset. Not associated with the docker/multi-arch-prebuild commits in messaging — it looks orphaned here.

**Untracked files (all dated May 14, ~a year ago)** that belong to a different line of work — decode-goal / max-ctx experiments:
- `dflash/baselines/` — ~20 JSON result files + `loop_decode_goal/` + summary docs
- `dflash/docs/GOAL_decode_tree_verify_max_ctx.md`
- `dflash/scripts/start_server.sh`, `dflash/scripts/decode_goal_retry_loop.sh`

These match the description of `stash@{0}: On integration/props-uv: decode-goal wip + baselines/docs/decode_goal_retry_loop`. They likely got dragged here when you switched branches.

**Stashes** — both belong to other branches:
- `stash@{0}` integration/props-uv — decode-goal wip
- `stash@{1}` feat/setup-results-uv — pre-rebase snapshot

**`.claude/`** is just harness worktree state — ignorable.

**My recommendation**: three separate concerns to capture before doing anything destructive —
1. The submodule `fattn.cu` changes (82 lines) — figure out which branch they belong to and commit there.
2. The May-14 decode-goal artifacts — these look like work that belongs on `integration/props-uv` (or successor), not here. Move/commit there or confirm `stash@{0}` already covers them.
3. The 5-vs-6 branch divergence — decide rebase-and-force-push vs. merge before pushing.

Want me to dig into any of these (e.g., diff the submodule change, compare untracked files against `stash@{0}`, or diff the local vs. remote commits)?
This is strange. We were working on an integration branch with benchmarks shared with bragi. Did we lose that?

> #!/usr/bin/env bash
> # Canonical launcher for the Luce DFlash OpenAI server on this rig.
> #
> # Config validated against live agent traffic on 2026-05-14. Each flag has
> # a reason; don't change one without re-running the corresponding sweep.
> #
> #   --max-ctx 98304
> #       Below the 120,320 capacity cliff documented in
> #       dflash/baselines/max_ctx_scaling_summary.md. Headroom for 64K+
> #       prompts. Going to 114688 was inside the safe envelope per the
> #       synthetic sweep but the live server hit roughly half the bench
> #       throughput, so we hold at 98K until that gap is understood.
> #
> #   --cache-type-k/v q4_0
> #       Explicit — server.py's default auto-selects tq3_0 at max_ctx>6144,
> #       which measured ~half the decode tok/s of q4_0 on real agent prompts.
> #       The dflash/baselines/ sweeps were also q4_0; staying on q4_0 keeps
> #       the deployed config consistent with the validated baseline.
> #
> #   --prefix-cache-slots 0
> #       Workaround. The inline-snapshot path hits an `empty_slot` abort
> #       under load (see /tmp/dflash_server_98304_q4.log). Disabling the
> #       cache trades the prefix-reuse speedup for reliability. Re-enable
> #       (default 1) once that path is fixed.
> #
> #   (no --lazy-draft)
> #       --lazy-draft adds ~1s of park/unpark to every request and was
> #       triggering client-side timeouts on streaming chat completions
> #       (46/50 requests aborted at 0 tokens during the first session).
> #       The full draft fits in VRAM at q4_0 KV + 98K ctx, so keep it warm.
> #
> #   --budget 22 / --fa-window 2048
> #       DDTree budget sweet spot and z-lab Qwen3.6 draft's SWA window.
> #
> # Logs:
> #   $DFLASH_SERVER_LOG  (default /tmp/dflash_server.log)
> #
> # To run in foreground (recommended for first launch — watch the readiness
> # line "Uvicorn running on http://0.0.0.0:1236"):

> # Canonical launcher for the Luce DFlash OpenAI server on this rig.
> #
> # Config validated against live agent traffic on 2026-05-14. Each flag has
> # a reason; don't change one without re-running the corresponding sweep.
> #
> #   --max-ctx 98304
> #       Below the 120,320 capacity cliff documented in
> #       dflash/baselines/max_ctx_scaling_summary.md. Headroom for 64K+
> #       prompts. Going to 114688 was inside the safe envelope per the
> #       synthetic sweep but the live server hit roughly half the bench
> #       throughput, so we hold at 98K until that gap is understood.
> #
> #   --cache-type-k/v q4_0
> #       Explicit — server.py's default auto-selects tq3_0 at max_ctx>6144,
> #       which measured ~half the decode tok/s of q4_0 on real agent prompts.
> #       The dflash/baselines/ sweeps were also q4_0; staying on q4_0 keeps
> #       the deployed config consistent with the validated baseline.
> #
> #   --prefix-cache-slots 0
> #       Workaround. The inline-snapshot path hits an `empty_slot` abort
> #       under load (see /tmp/dflash_server_98304_q4.log). Disabling the
> #       cache trades the prefix-reuse speedup for reliability. Re-enable
> #       (default 1) once that path is fixed.
> #
> #   (no --lazy-draft)
> #       --lazy-draft adds ~1s of park/unpark to every request and was
> #       triggering client-side timeouts on streaming chat completions
> #       (46/50 requests aborted at 0 tokens during the first session).
> #       The full draft fits in VRAM at q4_0 KV + 98K ctx, so keep it warm.
> #
> #   --budget 22 / --fa-window 2048
> #       DDTree budget sweet spot and z-lab Qwen3.6 draft's SWA window.
> #
> # Logs:
> #   $DFLASH_SERVER_LOG  (default /tmp/dflash_server.log)
> #
> # To run in foreground (recommended for first launch — watch the readiness
> # line "Uvicorn running on http://0.0.0.0:1236"):

> 
> ## Objective
>

> not advertised `--max-ctx` capacity, so agentic sessions can keep a large
> context window available without making shorter turns unusably slow.

> ## Current Evidence

## Findings

### 1. Submodule `fattn.cu` (82 lines) — **flash-attention dispatch tracer**
Pure instrumentation: prints kernel choice, Q/K/V shapes/types/strides, and mask shape per FA call. Gated by env vars:
- `DFLASH27B_FA_TRACE=1` — enable
- `DFLASH27B_FA_TRACE_MAX` — line limit (default 200)

This is a **debugging tool for the decode-goal max-ctx investigation** (matches the May-14 GOAL doc). Not a fix — a probe. Not on any submodule branch, not on `bench-agent-loop-faithful-replay` (that branch's submodule fixes are unrelated cuMem/TQ3 work, and its tip is already merged into our current submodule HEAD).

**Recommendation**: Save it on a feature branch inside the submodule (`feat/fa-dispatch-trace`) so it's recoverable, then reset the submodule. Or discard if the investigation is dead.

### 2. Stash@{0} ≠ the untracked May-14 files
Important correction to my earlier read: `git stash` only captured **tracked-file modifications** at the time:
- `dflash/scripts/bench_llm.py` (+389/-20)
- `dflash/test/test_dflash.cpp` (+21/-20)

The untracked files (`start_server.sh`, `decode_goal_retry_loop.sh`, GOAL doc, `dflash/baselines/`) **were never in the stash**. They exist **only on disk here, in no other branch or stash**. If you delete them, they're gone.

The stash's bench_llm.py mods (+389 lines — substantial) look like real bench work that may or may not have been incorporated into the easel/clean bench suite. Worth a diff before dropping `stash@{0}`.

### 3. Local clean is *older* content, not unique content
- Diff: 7,371 insertions / 976 deletions across 165 files between local and easel clean.
- **Zero files exist only on local**. Easel adds 12 files (gemma4 backend, disk_prefix_cache, tool_hint, gguf_mmap, etc. — all from rebased main).
- Key user-authored files differ in size, with easel always being the larger/newer version:

  - `bench_http_capability.py` +55/-31
  - `bench_ds4_eval.py` +10/-10
  - `thinking-budget.md` +103/-7
  - `Dockerfile` +5/-0
- Conclusion: **resetting local to easel/clean loses nothing.** Bragi's branch is strictly newer.

### 4. Additional flag: `bench-agent-loop-faithful-replay`
That branch's 8 unique commits are **faithful-replay bench work** (prefix cache phase A/B, transcript replay, empty-response repro, TTFT). The tip's submodule bump (`cuMem pool extension fix`) is already incorporated into our current submodule via easel PR #4 — *but* the prefix-cache bench scripts themselves are unique and don't appear in easel/clean's bench suite. Worth keeping or porting before deletion.

# max_ctx scaling baseline summary

Hardware: RTX 3090 Ti 24 GB.

Environment: `DFLASH27B_KV_Q4=1`, DDTree budget 22, `n_gen=64`,
default `DFLASH27B_FA_WINDOW=2048`.

## Current HEAD, before engine tuning

Synthetic repeated-token prompt:

| prompt tokens | max_ctx | prefill s | decode tok/s | AL | result |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|:---|
| 4,096 | fit=4,352 | 7.00 | 100.52 | 16.00 | pass |
| 4,096 | 32,768 | 7.03 | 81.52 | 16.00 | pass |
| 4,096 | 65,536 | 7.03 | 72.84 | 16.00 | pass |
| 4,096 | 131,072 | 7.06 | 24.37 | 16.00 | pass prefill, decode slow |

=== GOAL doc working diagnosis section ===

`DFLASH27B_FA_TRACE=1` shows tree verify attention now uses a window-sized mask
after the first fix:

```text
mask=[2080,32]
```

But K/V views still expose capacity-sized per-head stride:

```text
K_stride_tokens=131072
V_stride_tokens=131072
```

This suggests the hot path still pays for, or is memory-layout-limited by, the
persistent KV cache layout `[head_dim, max_ctx_alloc, n_head_kv]` even when the
logical FA window is about 2K tokens.

Later packing experiments refined this diagnosis: an env-gated same-type
contiguous K/V pack reduced the FA trace stride to the active window
(`K_stride_tokens=2071`), but synthetic 4K at `max_ctx=131072` stayed flat to
slightly worse (`23.04` tok/s versus `23.61` tok/s baseline). The corresponding
agent-history check was invalidated by a concurrent GPU training process. So
capacity-sized K/V stride alone is not currently proven to be the fix.

Bisection found a sharp Q4 capacity cliff between `119808` and `120320`.
`119808` stays near fitted-context decode throughput for synthetic 4K,
synthetic 32K, and agent-history 4K prompts. `120320` collapses to roughly
one-third of fitted throughput with unchanged prefix correctness and acceptance
length.

## Non-Goals

- Do not optimize PFlash compression quality or NIAH scoring in this goal.
- Do not change draft-model training or acceptance heuristics unless required
  to preserve correctness.
- Do not run multiple resident target copies to work around the issue.
- Do not regress fitted-context decode throughput to improve oversized-context
  throughput.

## Acceptance Criteria

Primary performance gate:

- Agent-history prompt, `prompt_tokens=4096`, `max_ctx=131072` reaches at least
  `0.75x` of fitted-context decode tok/s with identical generated-token prefix.
  Current post-mask-fix result is `4.61 / 23.42 = 0.20x`.

Secondary performance gates:

### May-14 files — **useful, save them**

The work was a real perf investigation:
- **GOAL doc**: bisected a sharp Q4 KV decode cliff between `max_ctx=119808` and `120320`; agent-history 4K prompt at max_ctx=131072 dropped to `0.20x` of fitted-context decode tok/s.
- **`dflash/baselines/`**: ~20 measured-on-RTX-3090-Ti JSONs + summary that back the doc's claims. Reusable as historical reference.
- **`decode_goal_retry_loop.sh`**: generic overnight-benchmark orchestrator (locking, GPU-busy detection, iteration state). Reusable beyond decode-goal.
- **`start_server.sh`**: dflash_server launcher with each flag's rationale documented from the sweeps. Lower-level than `lucebox.sh`/lucebox CLI.

Easel/clean **directionally addressed the same problem** with different fixes:
- `c4a7ba6 gemma4: implement G5 SWA ring-buffer, G6 fa_window, G3 compress`
- `570a71f server: add --fa-window CLI flag to dflash_server`
- `2ee7462 fix(scripts): auto-fit --max-ctx to prompt size`
- `c1bd053 fix(server): sync max_ctx between HTTP server and backend KV cache`

So the GOAL doc's *acceptance criteria* (≥0.75x fitted decode at max_ctx=131072) probably haven't been re-measured against the new stack. The baselines remain valuable as a "before" reference.

**Recommendation**: commit the May-14 files to a new branch `wip/decode-goal-may14` off `integration/props-uv` (where they originated per the stash description). They don't belong on easel/clean — different architecture — but they shouldn't die untracked either.

> #!/usr/bin/env bash
> # Canonical launcher for the Luce DFlash OpenAI server on this rig.
> #
> # Config validated against live agent traffic on 2026-05-14. Each flag has
> # a reason; don't change one without re-running the corresponding sweep.
> #
> #   --max-ctx 98304
> #       Below the 120,320 capacity cliff documented in
> #       dflash/baselines/max_ctx_scaling_summary.md. Headroom for 64K+
> #       prompts. Going to 114688 was inside the safe envelope per the
> #       synthetic sweep but the live server hit roughly half the bench
> #       throughput, so we hold at 98K until that gap is understood.
> #
> #   --cache-type-k/v q4_0
> #       Explicit — server.py's default auto-selects tq3_0 at max_ctx>6144,
> #       which measured ~half the decode tok/s of q4_0 on real agent prompts.
> #       The dflash/baselines/ sweeps were also q4_0; staying on q4_0 keeps
> #       the deployed config consistent with the validated baseline.
> #
> #   --prefix-cache-slots 0
> #       Workaround. The inline-snapshot path hits an `empty_slot` abort
> #       under load (see /tmp/dflash_server_98304_q4.log). Disabling the
> #       cache trades the prefix-reuse speedup for reliability. Re-enable
> #       (default 1) once that path is fixed.
> #
> #   (no --lazy-draft)
> #       --lazy-draft adds ~1s of park/unpark to every request and was
> #       triggering client-side timeouts on streaming chat completions
> #       (46/50 requests aborted at 0 tokens during the first session).
> #       The full draft fits in VRAM at q4_0 KV + 98K ctx, so keep it warm.
> #
> #   --budget 22 / --fa-window 2048
> #       DDTree budget sweet spot and z-lab Qwen3.6 draft's SWA window.
> #
> # Logs:
> #   $DFLASH_SERVER_LOG  (default /tmp/dflash_server.log)
> #
> # To run in foreground (recommended for first launch — watch the readiness
> # line "Uvicorn running on http://0.0.0.0:1236"):

> # Goal: Fix oversized max_ctx decode/tree-verify throughput
> 
> ## Objective
> 
> Make DFlash decode and DDTree verify throughput scale with live KV/window length,
> not advertised `--max-ctx` capacity, so agentic sessions can keep a large
> context window available without making shorter turns unusably slow.
> 
> ## Current Evidence
> 
> Baseline artifacts live in `dflash/baselines/`.
> 
> Measured on RTX 3090 Ti 24 GB with:
> 
> - `DFLASH27B_KV_Q4=1`
> - DDTree budget `22`
> - `n_gen=64`
> - default `DFLASH27B_FA_WINDOW=2048`
> 
> Synthetic repeated-token prompt:
> 
> | prompt tokens | max_ctx | prefill s | decode tok/s | AL |
> |---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
> | 4,096 | fit=4,352 | 7.00 | 100.52 | 16.00 |
> | 4,096 | 131,072 | 7.06 | 24.37 | 16.00 |
> | 32,768 | fit=33,024 | 58.92 | 77.05 | 16.00 |
> | 32,768 | 131,072 | 59.34 | 20.26 | 16.00 |
> 
> Agent-history prompt:
> 
> | prompt tokens | max_ctx | prefill s | decode tok/s | AL |
> |---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
> | 4,096 | fit=4,352 | 7.47 | 19.96 | 2.67 |
> | 4,096 | 131,072 | 7.18 | 4.15 | 2.67 |
> 
> Prefill is already effectively flat against `max_ctx`; the remaining issue is
> decode/tree-verify throughput collapse when `max_ctx` is oversized.
> 
> ## Working Diagnosis

### 1. Submodule `fattn.cu` (82 lines) — **flash-attention dispatch tracer**
Pure instrumentation: prints kernel choice, Q/K/V shapes/types/strides, and mask shape per FA call. Gated by env vars:
- `DFLASH27B_FA_TRACE=1` — enable
- `DFLASH27B_FA_TRACE_MAX` — line limit (default 200)

This is a **debugging tool for the decode-goal max-ctx investigation** (matches the May-14 GOAL doc). Not a fix — a probe. Not on any submodule branch, not on `bench-agent-loop-faithful-replay` (that branch's submodule fixes are unrelated cuMem/TQ3 work, and its tip is already merged into our current submodule HEAD).

**Recommendation**: Save it on a feature branch inside the submodule (`feat/fa-dispatch-trace`) so it's recoverable, then reset the submodule. Or discard if the investigation is dead.

### 2. Stash@{0} ≠ the untracked May-14 files

=== GOAL doc working diagnosis section ===

`DFLASH27B_FA_TRACE=1` shows tree verify attention now uses a window-sized mask
after the first fix:

```text
mask=[2080,32]
```

But K/V views still expose capacity-sized per-head stride:

```text
K_stride_tokens=131072
V_stride_tokens=131072
```

This suggests the hot path still pays for, or is memory-layout-limited by, the
persistent KV cache layout `[head_dim, max_ctx_alloc, n_head_kv]` even when the
logical FA window is about 2K tokens.

Later packing experiments refined this diagnosis: an env-gated same-type
contiguous K/V pack reduced the FA trace stride to the active window
(`K_stride_tokens=2071`), but synthetic 4K at `max_ctx=131072` stayed flat to
slightly worse (`23.04` tok/s versus `23.61` tok/s baseline). The corresponding
agent-history check was invalidated by a concurrent GPU training process. So
capacity-sized K/V stride alone is not currently proven to be the fix.

Bisection found a sharp Q4 capacity cliff between `119808` and `120320`.
`119808` stays near fitted-context decode throughput for synthetic 4K,
synthetic 32K, and agent-history 4K prompts. `120320` collapses to roughly
one-third of fitted throughput with unchanged prefix correctness and acceptance
length.

## Non-Goals

- Do not optimize PFlash compression quality or NIAH scoring in this goal.
- Do not change draft-model training or acceptance heuristics unless required
  to preserve correctness.
- Do not run multiple resident target copies to work around the issue.
- Do not regress fitted-context decode throughput to improve oversized-context
  throughput.

## Acceptance Criteria

Primary performance gate:

- Agent-history prompt, `prompt_tokens=4096`, `max_ctx=131072` reaches at least
  `0.75x` of fitted-context decode tok/s with identical generated-token prefix.
  Current post-mask-fix result is `4.61 / 23.42 = 0.20x`.

Secondary performance gates:

--- Header of the repro script ---
"""Standalone repro for the empty-response correctness regression in
dflash/scripts/server_tools.py + dflash/scripts/prefix_cache.py.

Sends a SEQUENCE of multi-turn chat completions to one warm dflash server.
Each call extends the conversation by one full
  assistant(tool_call) → tool(result) → user(continue)
turn, so the prefix grows naturally and the cache populates inline
snapshots between calls — the same pattern that
dflash/scripts/bench_agent_loop.py walks through transcripts.

Then runs the SAME sequence against a slots=0 server (cache disabled)
as the control.

The tool result is generated programmatically (synthetic pylint-like
output, deterministic per turn index) so this script has no dependency
on any private session transcript.

Expected: every call produces non-empty content. Observed against
PR #59: starting at some call N (often N=3 around 12-15K char prefix),
warm responses become content_len=0 / reasoning_len=0 / completion_tokens=0,
finish_reason="stop". The first broken call still does the prefill (slow
wall time) but emits nothing; subsequent calls hit the cache and return
empty in <50 ms.

Usage:
    python3 repro_empty_response.py \\
        --target /path/to/Qwen3.6-27B*.gguf \\
        --draft  /path/to/qwen3.6-27b-dflash \\
        --bin    /path/to/dflash/build/test_dflash \\
        --server /path/to/dflash/scripts/server_tools.py
"""

### Running on other GPUs (4090, 5090, DGX Spark / GB10, Jetson AGX Thor)

Supported out of the box; the build just needs the right CUDA toolkit. `dflash/CMakeLists.txt` already auto-adds Blackwell archs when your nvcc is new enough, so the main quickstart above works as-is on newer cards.

| GPU | Arch | Min CUDA | Status |
|-----|:----:|:--------:|--------|
| Tesla P40 Pascal | `sm_61` | 12.0 | supported with scalar F16 fallback; needs 24 GB for the 27B stack |
| Tesla V100 Volta | `sm_70` | 12.0 | supported with F16 WMMA kernels |
| RTX 3090 Ampere | `sm_86` | 12.0 | **reference, all numbers above** |
| RTX 2080 Ti Turing | `sm_75` | 12.0 | supported, 53 tok/s DFlash verified (FP16 draft) |
| RTX 4090 Ada | `sm_89` | 12.0 | should work, unverified, pass `-DCMAKE_CUDA_ARCHITECTURES=89` |
| RTX 5090 Blackwell consumer | `sm_120` | 12.8 | **205 tok/s DFlash, 4.84× vs AR** (Q4_K_M, budget=40) |
| DGX Spark / GB10 | `sm_121` (compute capability 12.1) | 12.9 | supported, auto-added by CMake |
| Jetson AGX Thor | `sm_110` | 13.0 | supported, auto-added by CMake |

Verify your target:
```bash
python -c "import torch; p=torch.cuda.get_device_properties(0); print(p.name, 'sm_%d%d'%(p.major,p.minor), p.multi_processor_count,'SMs', round(p.total_memory/1e9,1),'GB')"
nvcc --version
```

**DGX Spark / GB10 quick start:**
```bash
# CUDA 12.9+ required for sm_121
nvcc --version  # must show >= 12.9
git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/Luce-Org/lucebox-hub && cd lucebox-hub/dflash
cmake -B build -S . -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release   # CMake auto-adds sm_121
cmake --build build --target test_dflash -j
```

**Jetson AGX Thor quick start:**
```bash
# CUDA 13.0+ required for sm_110 / AGX Thor.
nvcc --version
git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/Luce-Org/lucebox-hub && cd lucebox-hub/dflash
cmake -B build -S . -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release   # CMake auto-adds the Thor arch your nvcc supports
cmake --build build --target test_dflash -j
```

**Retune per GPU:**
- **DDTree `budget=22`** tuned for 3090 + Q4_K_M + 24 GB. On the RTX 5090, budget=40 is optimal (swept). On GB10 (128 GB unified), re-sweep — larger tree = more verify throughput until memory bandwidth saturates. `scripts/bench_llm.py --budget N` has the sweep hooks.
- **TQ3_0 KV cache + sliding `target_feat` ring** was shaped by 24 GB (fits up to 256K context on a 3090). On GB10 (128 GB unified) / 5090 (32 GB) you can push context further or skip quantization entirely and keep F16 KV.
- **Perf numbers** (207 tok/s demo, 129.5 HumanEval, 2.8× vs SGLang AWQ) are RTX 3090 @ stock. RTX 5090 numbers (205 tok/s HumanEval, 4.84×) are in [RESULTS.md](dflash/RESULTS.md). Ada/GB10/Thor not yet swept, PRs with `RESULTS.md` entries welcome.

./test_dflash target.gguf draft.safetensors prompt.bin 128 out.bin \
  --draft-gpu=0 --target-gpus=0,1 --target-layer-split=1,3 \

When more than one target GPU is listed, `test_dflash` runs a non-daemon
target-only layer-split harness. Each target GPU loads only its assigned
contiguous layer range; `output_norm` and `output.weight` stay on the last
target GPU. `--target-split-load-draft` additionally loads the DFlash draft
on `--draft-gpu` (`.safetensors` or quantized draft `.gguf`), captures target
features into a draft-side mirror, and runs a small draft forward smoke. This
allows capacity checks where the draft and a target layer range share one GPU
before serving integration. `--target-split-dflash` runs the same split target
placement through a chain DFlash decode loop and reports acceptance length.

**Python flags on `scripts/run.py`, `scripts/server.py`:**
```bash
python3 scripts/run.py --ctk q8_0 --ctv q4_0 --prompt "hello"
python3 scripts/run.py --cache-type-k q8_0 --cache-type-v q4_0 --prompt "hello"
```

**TQ3 semantics under asymmetry:** TQ3_0 is K-side-driven. The FWHT rotation is applied to the query and inverse-applied to the attention output only when `K=TQ3_0`. V's type is independent of rotation. The 256-stride context alignment is triggered if *either* K or V is TQ3_0.

Legacy `--kv-tq3` / `--kv-q4` / `--kv-f16` flags continue to work as symmetric shorthand for backward compatibility.

## Qwen3.6-27B Target

Qwen3.6-27B is the default integration path. It uses the same `qwen35` target architecture as Qwen3.5, and the docs use the Lucebox GGUF DFlash draft by default.

```bash
# 1. target
hf download unsloth/Qwen3.6-27B-GGUF Qwen3.6-27B-Q4_K_M.gguf --local-dir models/

# 2. matched 3.6 draft (GGUF, used by default by scripts/run.py and server.py)
hf download Lucebox/Qwen3.6-27B-DFlash-GGUF dflash-draft-3.6-q8_0.gguf --local-dir models/draft/

# 3. bench
DFLASH_TARGET=models/Qwen3.6-27B-Q4_K_M.gguf python3 scripts/bench_he.py --n-gen 128
```

The default draft path is discovered under `models/draft/`. Scripts prefer `dflash-draft-*.gguf`, then any `.gguf`, then `model.safetensors`. Explicit `.gguf` and safetensors drafts still work via `DFLASH_DRAFT` / `--draft`; qwen35-compatible targets remain swappable via `DFLASH_TARGET` / `--target`.

## Native C++ HTTP server

`dflash_server` serves the same client-facing local API surface used by the
harnesses without the Python `scripts/server.py` wrapper. It supports `/health`,
`/v1/models`, OpenAI Chat Completions including streaming and tool metadata,

Header of bench_long_ctx.py:
"""
Long-context sweep: compare Q4_0 vs TQ3_0 KV cache at 32K / 64K / 128K.

Measures prefill time and DFlash decode tok/s at each context length under
both KV formats. The point is not throughput (TQ3 is slightly slower than
Q4_0 at short contexts) but memory — TQ3_0 (3.5 bpv) uses 22% less KV than
Q4_0 (4.5 bpv), enabling longer contexts on the same VRAM budget.

Usage:

### RTX 5090 Laptop 24 GB (sm_120) — CUDA 13.2, driver 581.80

| Task      | AR tok/s | DFlash tok/s | AL   | Speedup |
|-----------|:--------:|:------------:|:----:|:-------:|
| HumanEval | 23.96    | **87.30**    | 8.49 | **3.64×** |
| GSM8K     | 23.77    | **70.92**    | 6.92 | **2.98×** |
| Math500   | 23.77    | **72.97**    | 7.15 | **3.07×** |

AR is lower than on the 3090 (~24 vs ~38 tok/s) due to laptop power limits and memory bandwidth. The DFlash speedup ratio holds — HumanEval actually improves to 3.64× at AL 8.49, consistent with the draft having been distilled on Qwen3.5 hidden states which transfer across quantisation targets.

AR is lower than on the 3090 (~24 vs ~38 tok/s) due to laptop power limits and memory bandwidth. The DFlash speedup ratio holds — HumanEval actually improves to 3.64× at AL 8.49, consistent with the draft having been distilled on Qwen3.5 hidden states which transfer across quantisation targets.

### RTX 5090 Laptop — TQ3_0 KV cache (`DFLASH27B_KV_TQ3=1`)

| Task      | AR tok/s | DFlash tok/s | AL   | Speedup |
|-----------|:--------:|:------------:|:----:|:-------:|
| HumanEval | 24.09    | 78.91        | 7.76 | 3.28×   |
| GSM8K     | 23.95    | 64.75        | 6.40 | 2.70×   |
| Math500   | 24.01    | 67.85        | 6.57 | 2.83×   |

TQ3_0 (3.5 bpv) costs ~0.7 AL and ~10 tok/s vs the default KV format at short contexts. The memory saving (9.7× vs F16, vs 8× for Q4_0) is the point — TQ3 enables longer contexts on the same VRAM budget, not higher short-context throughput. A long-context sweep (32K–256K) comparing TQ3 vs Q4_0 on the 5090 has not been run yet.

# Luce DFlash benchmark results

Single RTX 3090 24 GB, CUDA 12, driver 535.
Target: `unsloth/Qwen3.5-27B-GGUF` (Q4_K_M, ~16 GB).
Draft:  `z-lab/Qwen3.5-27B-DFlash` (BF16, 3.46 GB).
Concurrency = 1, greedy decoding, `n_gen=256`.
Reproduce with `python3 scripts/bench_llm.py` (samples 10 prompts/dataset, seed=42).

## Headline — AR vs Luce DFlash at concurrency 1

| Task      | AR tok/s | DFlash tok/s | AL   | Speedup |
|-----------|:--------:|:------------:|:----:|:-------:|
| HumanEval | 37.78    | **129.52**   | 8.31 | **3.43×** |
| Math500   | 37.71    | **110.51**   | 7.04 | **2.93×** |
| GSM8K     | 37.65    | **96.15**    | 6.14 | **2.55×** |

AR = autoregressive target-only decode via `test_generate`.
DFlash = block-diffusion draft + DDTree budget 22 verify + fast rollback.
AL = mean committed tokens per draft/verify step (acceptance length).

Datasets pulled live via HuggingFace `datasets`:
- HumanEval — `openai_humaneval`, `prompt` field
- GSM8K    — `gsm8k` main split, `Question: … Answer: ` format
- Math500  — `HuggingFaceH4/MATH-500`, `Problem: … Solution: ` format

> #!/usr/bin/env bash
> # Canonical launcher for the Luce DFlash OpenAI server on this rig.
> #
> # Config validated against live agent traffic on 2026-05-14. Each flag has
> # a reason; don't change one without re-running the corresponding sweep.
> #
> #   --max-ctx 98304
> #       Below the 120,320 capacity cliff documented in
> #       dflash/baselines/max_ctx_scaling_summary.md. Headroom for 64K+
> #       prompts. Going to 114688 was inside the safe envelope per the
> #       synthetic sweep but the live server hit roughly half the bench
> #       throughput, so we hold at 98K until that gap is understood.
> #
> #   --cache-type-k/v q4_0
> #       Explicit — server.py's default auto-selects tq3_0 at max_ctx>6144,
> #       which measured ~half the decode tok/s of q4_0 on real agent prompts.
> #       The dflash/baselines/ sweeps were also q4_0; staying on q4_0 keeps
> #       the deployed config consistent with the validated baseline.
> #
> #   --prefix-cache-slots 0
> #       Workaround. The inline-snapshot path hits an `empty_slot` abort
> #       under load (see /tmp/dflash_server_98304_q4.log). Disabling the
> #       cache trades the prefix-reuse speedup for reliability. Re-enable
> #       (default 1) once that path is fixed.
> #
> #   (no --lazy-draft)
> #       --lazy-draft adds ~1s of park/unpark to every request and was
> #       triggering client-side timeouts on streaming chat completions
> #       (46/50 requests aborted at 0 tokens during the first session).
> #       The full draft fits in VRAM at q4_0 KV + 98K ctx, so keep it warm.
> #
> #   --budget 22 / --fa-window 2048
> #       DDTree budget sweet spot and z-lab Qwen3.6 draft's SWA window.
> #
> # Logs:
> #   $DFLASH_SERVER_LOG  (default /tmp/dflash_server.log)
> #
> # To run in foreground (recommended for first launch — watch the readiness
> # line "Uvicorn running on http://0.0.0.0:1236"):

## Findings

### 1. Submodule `fattn.cu` (82 lines) — **flash-attention dispatch tracer**
Pure instrumentation: prints kernel choice, Q/K/V shapes/types/strides, and mask shape per FA call. Gated by env vars:
- `DFLASH27B_FA_TRACE=1` — enable
- `DFLASH27B_FA_TRACE_MAX` — line limit (default 200)

This is a **debugging tool for the decode-goal max-ctx investigation** (matches the May-14 GOAL doc). Not a fix — a probe. Not on any submodule branch, not on `bench-agent-loop-faithful-replay` (that branch's submodule fixes are unrelated cuMem/TQ3 work, and its tip is already merged into our current submodule HEAD).

**Recommendation**: Save it on a feature branch inside the submodule (`feat/fa-dispatch-trace`) so it's recoverable, then reset the submodule. Or discard if the investigation is dead.

### 2. Stash@{0} ≠ the untracked May-14 files
Important correction to my earlier read: `git stash` only captured **tracked-file modifications** at the time:
- `dflash/scripts/bench_llm.py` (+389/-20)
- `dflash/test/test_dflash.cpp` (+21/-20)

The untracked files (`start_server.sh`, `decode_goal_retry_loop.sh`, GOAL doc, `dflash/baselines/`) **were never in the stash**. They exist **only on disk here, in no other branch or stash**. If you delete them, they're gone.

The stash's bench_llm.py mods (+389 lines — substantial) look like real bench work that may or may not have been incorporated into the easel/clean bench suite. Worth a diff before dropping `stash@{0}`.

### 3. Local clean is *older* content, not unique content
- Diff: 7,371 insertions / 976 deletions across 165 files between local and easel clean.
- **Zero files exist only on local**. Easel adds 12 files (gemma4 backend, disk_prefix_cache, tool_hint, gguf_mmap, etc. — all from rebased main).

That branch's 8 unique commits are **faithful-replay bench work** (prefix cache phase A/B, transcript replay, empty-response repro, TTFT). The tip's submodule bump (`cuMem pool extension fix`) is already incorporated into our current submodule via easel PR #4 — *but* the prefix-cache bench scripts themselves are unique and don't appear in easel/clean's bench suite. Worth keeping or porting before deletion.

# max_ctx scaling baseline summary

Hardware: RTX 3090 Ti 24 GB.

Environment: `DFLASH27B_KV_Q4=1`, DDTree budget 22, `n_gen=64`,
default `DFLASH27B_FA_WINDOW=2048`.

## Current HEAD, before engine tuning

Synthetic repeated-token prompt:

| prompt tokens | max_ctx | prefill s | decode tok/s | AL | result |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|:---|
| 4,096 | fit=4,352 | 7.00 | 100.52 | 16.00 | pass |
| 4,096 | 32,768 | 7.03 | 81.52 | 16.00 | pass |
| 4,096 | 65,536 | 7.03 | 72.84 | 16.00 | pass |
| 4,096 | 131,072 | 7.06 | 24.37 | 16.00 | pass prefill, decode slow |
| 32,768 | fit=33,024 | 58.92 | 77.05 | 16.00 | pass |
| 32,768 | 131,072 | 59.34 | 20.26 | 16.00 | pass prefill, decode slow |

=== GOAL doc working diagnosis section ===

`DFLASH27B_FA_TRACE=1` shows tree verify attention now uses a window-sized mask
after the first fix:

```text
mask=[2080,32]
```

But K/V views still expose capacity-sized per-head stride:

```text
K_stride_tokens=131072
V_stride_tokens=131072
```

This suggests the hot path still pays for, or is memory-layout-limited by, the
persistent KV cache layout `[head_dim, max_ctx_alloc, n_head_kv]` even when the
logical FA window is about 2K tokens.

Later packing experiments refined this diagnosis: an env-gated same-type
contiguous K/V pack reduced the FA trace stride to the active window
(`K_stride_tokens=2071`), but synthetic 4K at `max_ctx=131072` stayed flat to
slightly worse (`23.04` tok/s versus `23.61` tok/s baseline). The corresponding
agent-history check was invalidated by a concurrent GPU training process. So
capacity-sized K/V stride alone is not currently proven to be the fix.

Bisection found a sharp Q4 capacity cliff between `119808` and `120320`.
`119808` stays near fitted-context decode throughput for synthetic 4K,
synthetic 32K, and agent-history 4K prompts. `120320` collapses to roughly
one-third of fitted throughput with unchanged prefix correctness and acceptance
length.

## Non-Goals

- Do not optimize PFlash compression quality or NIAH scoring in this goal.
- Do not change draft-model training or acceptance heuristics unless required
  to preserve correctness.

- **`decode_goal_retry_loop.sh`**: generic overnight-benchmark orchestrator (locking, GPU-busy detection, iteration state). Reusable beyond decode-goal.
- **`start_server.sh`**: dflash_server launcher with each flag's rationale documented from the sweeps. Lower-level than `lucebox.sh`/lucebox CLI.

Easel/clean **directionally addressed the same problem** with different fixes:

So the GOAL doc's *acceptance criteria* (≥0.75x fitted decode at max_ctx=131072) probably haven't been re-measured against the new stack. The baselines remain valuable as a "before" reference.

[tool result] === bench-agent-loop: is the repro_empty_response.py useful, or covered elsewhere? ===
--- Header of the repro script ---
"""Standalone repro for the empty-response correctness regression in
dflash/scripts/server_tools.py + dflash/scripts/prefix_cache.py.

Sends a SEQUENCE of multi-turn chat completions to one warm dflash server.
Each call extends the conversation by one full
  assistant(tool_call) → tool(result) → user(continue)
turn, so the prefix grows naturally and the cache populates inline
snapshots between calls — the same pattern that
dflash/scripts/bench_agent_loop.py walks through transcripts.

Then runs the SAME sequence against a slots=0 server (cache disabled)
as the control.

The tool result is generated programmatically (synthetic pylint-like
output, deterministic per turn index) so this script has no dependency
on any private session transcript.

Expected: every call produces non-empty content. Observed against
PR #59: starting at some call N (often N=3 around 12-15K char prefix),
warm responses become content_len=0 / reasoning_len=0 / completion_tokens=0,
finish_reason="stop". The first broken call still does the prefill (slow
wall time) but emits nothing; subsequent calls hit the cache and return
empty in <50 ms.

Usage:
    python3 repro_empty_response.py \\
        --target /path/to/Qwen3.6-27B*.gguf \\
        --draft  /path/to/qwen3.6-27b-dflash \\
        --bin    /path/to/dflash/build/test_dflash \\
        --server /path/to/dflash/scripts/server_tools.py
"""
import argparse
import json
import signal

import sys
import time
import urllib.error

### Running on other GPUs (4090, 5090, DGX Spark / GB10, Jetson AGX Thor)

Supported out of the box; the build just needs the right CUDA toolkit. `dflash/CMakeLists.txt` already auto-adds Blackwell archs when your nvcc is new enough, so the main quickstart above works as-is on newer cards.

| GPU | Arch | Min CUDA | Status |
|-----|:----:|:--------:|--------|
| Tesla P40 Pascal | `sm_61` | 12.0 | supported with scalar F16 fallback; needs 24 GB for the 27B stack |
| Tesla V100 Volta | `sm_70` | 12.0 | supported with F16 WMMA kernels |
| RTX 3090 Ampere | `sm_86` | 12.0 | **reference, all numbers above** |
| RTX 2080 Ti Turing | `sm_75` | 12.0 | supported, 53 tok/s DFlash verified (FP16 draft) |
| RTX 4090 Ada | `sm_89` | 12.0 | should work, unverified, pass `-DCMAKE_CUDA_ARCHITECTURES=89` |
| RTX 5090 Blackwell consumer | `sm_120` | 12.8 | **205 tok/s DFlash, 4.84× vs AR** (Q4_K_M, budget=40) |
| DGX Spark / GB10 | `sm_121` (compute capability 12.1) | 12.9 | supported, auto-added by CMake |
| Jetson AGX Thor | `sm_110` | 13.0 | supported, auto-added by CMake |

Verify your target:
```bash
python -c "import torch; p=torch.cuda.get_device_properties(0); print(p.name, 'sm_%d%d'%(p.major,p.minor), p.multi_processor_count,'SMs', round(p.total_memory/1e9,1),'GB')"
nvcc --version
```

**DGX Spark / GB10 quick start:**
```bash
# CUDA 12.9+ required for sm_121
nvcc --version  # must show >= 12.9
git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/Luce-Org/lucebox-hub && cd lucebox-hub/dflash
cmake -B build -S . -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release   # CMake auto-adds sm_121
cmake --build build --target test_dflash -j
```

**Jetson AGX Thor quick start:**
```bash
# CUDA 13.0+ required for sm_110 / AGX Thor.
nvcc --version
git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/Luce-Org/lucebox-hub && cd lucebox-hub/dflash
cmake -B build -S . -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release   # CMake auto-adds the Thor arch your nvcc supports
cmake --build build --target test_dflash -j
```

**Retune per GPU:**
- **DDTree `budget=22`** tuned for 3090 + Q4_K_M + 24 GB. On the RTX 5090, budget=40 is optimal (swept). On GB10 (128 GB unified), re-sweep — larger tree = more verify throughput until memory bandwidth saturates. `scripts/bench_llm.py --budget N` has the sweep hooks.
- **TQ3_0 KV cache + sliding `target_feat` ring** was shaped by 24 GB (fits up to 256K context on a 3090). On GB10 (128 GB unified) / 5090 (32 GB) you can push context further or skip quantization entirely and keep F16 KV.
- **Perf numbers** (207 tok/s demo, 129.5 HumanEval, 2.8× vs SGLang AWQ) are RTX 3090 @ stock. RTX 5090 numbers (205 tok/s HumanEval, 4.84×) are in [RESULTS.md](dflash/RESULTS.md). Ada/GB10/Thor not yet swept, PRs with `RESULTS.md` entries welcome.

./test_dflash target.gguf draft.safetensors prompt.bin 128 out.bin \
  --draft-gpu=0 --target-gpus=0,1 --target-layer-split=1,3 \

  --draft-gpu=0 --target-gpus=0,1 --target-layer-split=1,1 \
  --target-split-dflash
```

When more than one target GPU is listed, `test_dflash` runs a non-daemon
target-only layer-split harness. Each target GPU loads only its assigned
contiguous layer range; `output_norm` and `output.weight` stay on the last
target GPU. `--target-split-load-draft` additionally loads the DFlash draft
on `--draft-gpu` (`.safetensors` or quantized draft `.gguf`), captures target
features into a draft-side mirror, and runs a small draft forward smoke. This
allows capacity checks where the draft and a target layer range share one GPU
before serving integration. `--target-split-dflash` runs the same split target
placement through a chain DFlash decode loop and reports acceptance length.

**Python flags on `scripts/run.py`, `scripts/server.py`:**
```bash
python3 scripts/run.py --ctk q8_0 --ctv q4_0 --prompt "hello"
python3 scripts/run.py --cache-type-k q8_0 --cache-type-v q4_0 --prompt "hello"
```

**TQ3 semantics under asymmetry:** TQ3_0 is K-side-driven. The FWHT rotation is applied to the query and inverse-applied to the attention output only when `K=TQ3_0`. V's type is independent of rotation. The 256-stride context alignment is triggered if *either* K or V is TQ3_0.

Legacy `--kv-tq3` / `--kv-q4` / `--kv-f16` flags continue to work as symmetric shorthand for backward compatibility.

## Qwen3.6-27B Target

Qwen3.6-27B is the default integration path. It uses the same `qwen35` target architecture as Qwen3.5, and the docs use the Lucebox GGUF DFlash draft by default.

```bash
# 1. target
hf download unsloth/Qwen3.6-27B-GGUF Qwen3.6-27B-Q4_K_M.gguf --local-dir models/

# 2. matched 3.6 draft (GGUF, used by default by scripts/run.py and server.py)
hf download Lucebox/Qwen3.6-27B-DFlash-GGUF dflash-draft-3.6-q8_0.gguf --local-dir models/draft/

# 3. bench
DFLASH_TARGET=models/Qwen3.6-27B-Q4_K_M.gguf python3 scripts/bench_he.py --n-gen 128
```

The default draft path is discovered under `models/draft/`. Scripts prefer `dflash-draft-*.gguf`, then any `.gguf`, then `model.safetensors`. Explicit `.gguf` and safetensors drafts still work via `DFLASH_DRAFT` / `--draft`; qwen35-compatible targets remain swappable via `DFLASH_TARGET` / `--target`.

## Native C++ HTTP server

`dflash_server` serves the same client-facing local API surface used by the
harnesses without the Python `scripts/server.py` wrapper. It supports `/health`,
`/v1/models`, OpenAI Chat Completions including streaming and tool metadata,

# Luce DFlash benchmark results

Single RTX 3090 24 GB, CUDA 12, driver 535.
Target: `unsloth/Qwen3.5-27B-GGUF` (Q4_K_M, ~16 GB).
Draft:  `z-lab/Qwen3.5-27B-DFlash` (BF16, 3.46 GB).
Concurrency = 1, greedy decoding, `n_gen=256`.
Reproduce with `python3 scripts/bench_llm.py` (samples 10 prompts/dataset, seed=42).

## Headline — AR vs Luce DFlash at concurrency 1

| Task      | AR tok/s | DFlash tok/s | AL   | Speedup |
|-----------|:--------:|:------------:|:----:|:-------:|
| HumanEval | 37.78    | **129.52**   | 8.31 | **3.43×** |
| Math500   | 37.71    | **110.51**   | 7.04 | **2.93×** |
| GSM8K     | 37.65    | **96.15**    | 6.14 | **2.55×** |

AR = autoregressive target-only decode via `test_generate`.
DFlash = block-diffusion draft + DDTree budget 22 verify + fast rollback.
AL = mean committed tokens per draft/verify step (acceptance length).

- HumanEval — `openai_humaneval`, `prompt` field
- GSM8K    — `gsm8k` main split, `Question: … Answer: ` format
- Math500  — `HuggingFaceH4/MATH-500`, `Problem: … Solution: ` format

## ds4 benchmark status

### Current results (May 20, 2026)
Three completed runs of the full 92-case `quality.ds4_eval` (antirez/ds4 port), all identical:

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Strict pass | **16 / 92 = 17.39%** |
| Format pass | 92 / 92 = 100% |
| Semantic hint | 24 / 92 = 26% |
| Semantic pass | 0 / 92 = 0% |
| Thinking | **disabled** |
| max_tokens | **512** |

**Step gated as `failed`** in the profile snapshot (step #4, duration 32 min, completed_at 2026-05-20T16:44:07Z).

### Pattern in the failures
- **AIME2025** (math, integer answers): all failing — model emits "1", "2", "24", "196" etc. vs correct answers like 113, 510, 19, 248. Off by orders of magnitude — looks like it's truncating before reaching the answer or just stabbing at it.
- **GPQA Diamond / SuperGPQA** (multi-choice physics/science): nearly all failing — format-valid letters (A/C/J/G) but wrong choices.
- **COMPSEC** (numeric ranges): mixed — model passes about half (077-079, 085, 088, 089, 091).

Format is perfect, content is bad. Looks classic "model can't reason without `<think>` and max_tokens is tight."

### Two interrupted runs (May 21)
Both `00f7d99...` (01:48) and `de55dd6...` (00:31) were `KeyboardInterrupt` mid-run from `bench_http_capability.py` — never produced `bench-ds4-eval.json`. So no fresher data than the May-20 baseline.

### Active thinking-budget work
Bragi has been landing C++ thinking-budget infra over the past few days, including today's push:
- `8d6ff04 feat(cpp-server): thinking-budget surface (--think-max-tokens, finish_details)`
- `b4b46a4 feat(cpp-server): Level 1 thinking-budget reprompt + codex review fixes`
- `2a89e62 fix(cpp-server): pre-open <think> when enable_thinking=true (Qwen3.6)` (today)
- `5b67cf2 feat(dflash): align server props and thinking controls`

The bench's own docstring confirms `DS4_EVAL_MAX_TOKENS = 16000` is the upstream default that "covers reasoning + reply" — meaning the May-20 run at 512 is **way** below the budget the eval expects. That's a config issue, not a model capability ceiling.

### Bottom line
- 17.39% is a **non-thinking, severely token-capped baseline** — not a real measurement of model capability.
- The thinking-budget plumbing landed in the last 48 hours and **hasn't been re-benched** against ds4 yet.
- The two May 21 runs were likely the attempted re-bench with thinking on, both aborted.

### Suggested next steps
1. **Re-run with thinking enabled + max_tokens ~16000** to get a real number — that's what the eval was designed for. Expect a large jump from 17%; published Qwen3.6 numbers on similar benchmarks are 60-80%+ with reasoning.
2. **Frontiers/perf**: the May-20 snapshot has ttft/decode_tps for 2K/4K/8K/16K context. Decode is 30 → 11 tok/s from 2K → 16K — useful baseline.
3. **`bench-ds4-frontiers.csv` in dflash/models/.lucebox/** is a stale single-row 2026-05-17 artifact (only target=512). Either overwrite with the May-20 4-row sweep from the profile snapshot or just delete.

=== Top of lucebox.sh wrapper ===
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# lucebox.sh — host-side wrapper for the lucebox-hub container.
#
# Two jobs:
#
#   1) Probe the host (driver, docker, NVIDIA Container Toolkit, VRAM, RAM,
#      systemd), select the CUDA 12 image, and
#      dispatch into the in-container Python CLI via `docker run`. The
#      Python CLI lives at /opt/lucebox-hub/lucebox/ inside the image and is
#      the single source of truth for orchestration logic — TOML config,
#      autotune rules, server bring-up, benchmark sweeps, smoke tests, model
#      downloads.
#
#   2) Manage a user-level systemd unit (~/.config/systemd/user/lucebox.service)
#      so the server can run as a long-lived service without keeping a shell
#      open. install/uninstall/start/stop/enable/disable/status/logs all
#      delegate to systemctl --user / journalctl --user.
#
# Install:
#   curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Luce-Org/lucebox-hub/main/lucebox.sh \
#        -o ~/.local/bin/lucebox.sh && chmod +x ~/.local/bin/lucebox.sh
#
# Then: lucebox.sh check && lucebox.sh install && lucebox.sh start
#
# No root is ever taken automatically. Anything that needs sudo (package
# install, loginctl enable-linger) is printed for the user to run.

set -euo pipefail

VERSION="0.2.0"
SCRIPT_PATH="$(readlink -f "$0" 2>/dev/null || realpath "$0" 2>/dev/null || echo "$0")"
SCRIPT_NAME="$(basename "$SCRIPT_PATH")"

# ── tunables / env overrides ───────────────────────────────────────────────
IMAGE_BASE="${LUCEBOX_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/luce-org/lucebox-hub}"
CONTAINER_NAME="${LUCEBOX_CONTAINER:-lucebox}"
DEFAULT_PORT="${LUCEBOX_PORT:-8080}"
UNIT_NAME="lucebox.service"
UNIT_PATH="${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/systemd/user/$UNIT_NAME"

# CUDA driver floor for the prebuilt CUDA 12 image.
# shellcheck disable=SC2034
MIN_DRIVER_CUDA12=525

# ── output helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
if [ -t 1 ] && [ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]; then
    C_INFO='\033[1;34m'; C_OK='\033[1;32m'; C_WARN='\033[1;33m'
    C_ERR='\033[1;31m'; C_DIM='\033[2m'; C_RST='\033[0m'
else
    C_INFO=''; C_OK=''; C_WARN=''; C_ERR=''; C_DIM=''; C_RST=''

Confirmed from antirez/ds4 `ds4_eval.c` upstream:

```c
eval_config c = {
    ...
    .max_tokens = 16000,
    ...
    .soft_limit_reply_budget = 1024,
    .hard_limit_reply_budget = 512,
    ...
};

// later:
ui->max_tokens = generation_limit;  // = 16000
ui->think_max_tokens = generation_limit - cfg->hard_limit_reply_budget;  // = 15488
```

So upstream defaults are:
- **`max_tokens = 16000`** ← matches my fix
- **`think_max_tokens = 15488`** (computed as `16000 - hard_limit_reply_budget=512`)
- `soft_limit_reply_budget = 1024` (soft cap reserved for the reply once thinking closes)
- `hard_limit_reply_budget = 512` (hard floor — never let reply have less than this)

The wire-level `max_tokens=16000` is right. The server-side `--think-max-tokens` is a separate knob; upstream's behavior is "let thinking use up to 15488, reserve 512 for the reply." If our dflash server is configured with a much smaller `--think-max-tokens` (or doesn't reserve any reply budget), it won't behave the same way even with the 16000 wire cap fixed.

Worth checking what `--think-max-tokens` the server is launched with in your lucebox config before the re-run. If you want, I can pull that up.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# In-container ENTRYPOINT for lucebox-hub.
#
# Normal path: the host-side `lucebox` CLI has already populated every
# DFLASH_* env var from its detection / benchmark, so this script just
# resolves paths and execs the native dflash_server binary.
#
# Fallback path: a user runs the image directly (`docker run --gpus all
# ghcr.io/luce-org/lucebox-hub:cuda12`) with no env-var prep. We then do a
# minimal VRAM-tiered autotune — same tiers as `lucebox configure`, kept in
# sync by hand. Anything more elaborate (driver-version probes, AMD paths,
# lspci fallbacks) belongs in the host CLI, not here.

set -euo pipefail

DFLASH_DIR="/opt/lucebox-hub/dflash"

info()  { printf '\033[1;34m[INFO]\033[0m  %s\n' "$*"; }
warn()  { printf '\033[1;33m[WARN]\033[0m  %s\n' "$*"; }
die()   { printf '\033[1;31m[ERROR]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; exit 1; }

# ── arg dispatch ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# `serve` (default) — start the OpenAI-compatible server.
# `benchmark`        — run the in-container sweep (lucebox_bench.py).
# `shell`            — drop into bash inside the container (debug).
# `lucebox`          — dispatch to the Python CLI. Any subcommand
#                      `lucebox.sh` doesn't handle on the host arrives here
#                      (check, configure, pull, print-run, smoke, …).
# `python` or anything else
#                    — pass through to exec, so `docker run … python -m foo`
#                      still works for dev.
SUBCMD="${1:-serve}"
[ $# -gt 0 ] && shift || true

LUCEBOX_PKG="/opt/lucebox-hub"

case "$SUBCMD" in
    lucebox)
        exec uv run --directory "$LUCEBOX_PKG" python -m lucebox "$@"
        ;;
    benchmark)
        export DFLASH_DIR
        cd "$DFLASH_DIR"
        exec uv run --directory "$DFLASH_DIR" python scripts/lucebox_bench.py "$@"
        ;;
    shell)
        exec /bin/bash "$@"
        ;;
    serve|"")
        : # fall through to server startup below
        ;;
    *)
        exec "$SUBCMD" "$@"
        ;;
esac

# ── detect ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# nvidia-smi is always present here (--gpus all wires the driver in).
GPU_VRAM_GB=0
if command -v nvidia-smi &>/dev/null; then
    if mem_mib=$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=memory.total --format=csv,noheader,nounits 2>/dev/null \
                  | head -1) && [ -n "$mem_mib" ]; then
        GPU_VRAM_GB=$((mem_mib / 1024))
    fi
fi
GPU_COUNT=0
if command -v nvidia-smi &>/dev/null; then
    GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi -L 2>/dev/null | awk '/^GPU /{n++} END{print n+0}') || GPU_COUNT=0
fi

# ── fallback autotune (only fills unset env) ───────────────────────────────
# Keep these tiers in lockstep with lucebox::autotune_env on the host. The
# divergence we accept is the lower-VRAM error tier — the host CLI refuses
# to start there with a clear message; here we just warn and let the server
# decide whether it can load.

if [ "$GPU_VRAM_GB" -gt 0 ]; then
    IS_WSL=0
    if grep -qi microsoft /proc/version 2>/dev/null || [ -e /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/WSLInterop ]; then
        IS_WSL=1
    fi
    if [ "$GPU_VRAM_GB" -lt 12 ]; then
        : "${DFLASH_LAZY:=1}"
        : "${DFLASH_MAX_CTX:=4096}"
        warn "VRAM ${GPU_VRAM_GB} GB < 12 GB — 27B target unlikely to fit"
    elif [ "$GPU_VRAM_GB" -lt 22 ]; then
        : "${DFLASH_LAZY:=1}"
        : "${DFLASH_MAX_CTX:=32768}"
    elif [ "$GPU_VRAM_GB" -lt 32 ]; then
        : "${DFLASH_LAZY:=1}"
        if [ "$IS_WSL" = "1" ]; then
            : "${DFLASH_BUDGET:=16}"
            : "${DFLASH_MAX_CTX:=65536}"
        else
            : "${DFLASH_MAX_CTX:=98304}"
        fi
    elif [ "$GPU_VRAM_GB" -lt 48 ]; then
        : "${DFLASH_MAX_CTX:=131072}"
    else
        : "${DFLASH_PREFIX_CACHE_SLOTS:=0}"
        : "${DFLASH_MAX_CTX:=131072}"
    fi
fi

: "${DFLASH_BIN:=$DFLASH_DIR/build/test_dflash}"
: "${DFLASH_SERVER_BIN:=$DFLASH_DIR/build/dflash_server}"
: "${DFLASH_HOST:=0.0.0.0}"
: "${DFLASH_PORT:=8080}"
: "${DFLASH_BUDGET:=22}"
: "${DFLASH_MAX_CTX:=16384}"
: "${DFLASH_LAZY:=0}"
: "${DFLASH_PREFIX_CACHE_SLOTS:=0}"
: "${DFLASH_PREFILL_CACHE_SLOTS:=0}"
: "${DFLASH_CACHE_TYPE_K:=}"
: "${DFLASH_CACHE_TYPE_V:=}"
: "${DFLASH_VERBOSE:=0}"
: "${DFLASH_TARGET:=}"
: "${DFLASH_DRAFT:=$DFLASH_DIR/models/draft}"
: "${DFLASH_PREFILL_MODE:=off}"
: "${DFLASH_PREFILL_KEEP:=0.05}"
: "${DFLASH_PREFILL_THRESHOLD:=32000}"
: "${DFLASH_PREFILL_DRAFTER:=}"

# ── auto-detect target ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Largest .gguf wins (target ~16 GB vs ~1 GB drafter). Follow symlinks so a
# symlinked 27B target is ranked by target size instead of being skipped.
if [ -z "$DFLASH_TARGET" ] && [ -d "$DFLASH_DIR/models" ]; then
    # Prefer the canonical Qwen3.6 Q4_K_M target when several same-sized
    # variants are present; otherwise fall back to largest GGUF.
    DFLASH_TARGET=$(find -L "$DFLASH_DIR/models" -maxdepth 4 -type f \
                      -name '*Qwen3.6-27B-Q4_K_M.gguf' \
                      -printf '%s %p\n' 2>/dev/null \
                      | sort -nr | head -1 | awk '{ $1=""; sub(/^ /,""); print }')
    if [ -z "$DFLASH_TARGET" ]; then
        DFLASH_TARGET=$(find -L "$DFLASH_DIR/models" -maxdepth 4 -type f \
                          -name '*Qwen3.6*Q4_K_M*.gguf' \
                          -printf '%s %p\n' 2>/dev/null \
                          | sort -nr | head -1 | awk '{ $1=""; sub(/^ /,""); print }')
    fi
    if [ -z "$DFLASH_TARGET" ]; then
        DFLASH_TARGET=$(find -L "$DFLASH_DIR/models" -maxdepth 4 -type f -name '*.gguf' \
                          -printf '%s %p\n' 2>/dev/null \
                          | sort -nr | head -1 | awk '{ $1=""; sub(/^ /,""); print }')
    fi
fi

if [ -z "$DFLASH_TARGET" ] || [ ! -f "$DFLASH_TARGET" ]; then
    die "No target GGUF found. Mount a model dir: -v /host/models:/opt/lucebox-hub/dflash/models"
fi
[ -x "$DFLASH_SERVER_BIN" ] || die "dflash_server binary missing at $DFLASH_SERVER_BIN (image build failed?)"

# Qwen3.6 DFlash drafters use sliding-window attention in the draft. Some GGUFs
# carry this metadata directly; keep the documented env override as the startup
# default so older drafts behave like the benchmark path.
case "$(basename "$DFLASH_TARGET")" in
    *Qwen3.6*|*qwen3.6*)
        if [ -z "${DFLASH27B_DRAFT_SWA:-}" ]; then
            export DFLASH27B_DRAFT_SWA=2048
            info "Autotune: DFLASH27B_DRAFT_SWA=2048 (Qwen3.6 draft SWA)"
        fi
        ;;
esac

# Common host layouts use ~/models/qwen3.6-27b-dflash as an absolute symlink
# rather than a literal models/draft directory. If the default is absent, find
# that draft before deciding to run without DFlash.
if [ "$DFLASH_DRAFT" = "$DFLASH_DIR/models/draft" ] && [ ! -e "$DFLASH_DRAFT" ]; then
    for cand in "$DFLASH_DIR/models/qwen3.6-27b-dflash" \
                "$DFLASH_DIR/models/Qwen3.6-27B-DFlash" \
                "$DFLASH_DIR/models/dflash"; do
        if [ -e "$cand" ]; then
            DFLASH_DRAFT="$cand"
            break
        fi
    done
fi

# Draft: directory holding GGUF/safetensors, or a direct draft file.
# The native dflash_server expects --draft to be a FILE path (not a dir).
# If DFLASH_DRAFT points at a directory, resolve it to the first DFlash
# draft GGUF inside (preferring the size-based largest dflash-draft-*.gguf
# match). The Python server's resolve_draft() did the equivalent walk; this
# block makes the C++ entrypoint accept the same DFLASH_DRAFT shape so
# users don't have to know whether they're driving the Python or C++ path.
DRAFT_ARG="$DFLASH_DRAFT"
if [ -d "$DFLASH_DRAFT" ]; then
    DRAFT_FILE="$(find -L "$DFLASH_DRAFT" -maxdepth 4 -type f \( \
            -name 'dflash-draft-*.gguf' -o \
            -name '*.gguf' -o \
            -name 'model.safetensors' -o \
            -name '*.safetensors' \
        \) -printf '%s\t%p\n' 2>/dev/null | sort -rn | head -n1 | cut -f2)"
    if [ -n "$DRAFT_FILE" ] && [ -f "$DRAFT_FILE" ]; then
        DRAFT_ARG="$DRAFT_FILE"
        info "Resolved draft dir $DFLASH_DRAFT → $DRAFT_ARG"
    else
        warn "No DFlash draft GGUF/safetensors in draft dir $DFLASH_DRAFT — running without draft"
        DRAFT_ARG=""
    fi
elif [ -n "$DFLASH_DRAFT" ] && [ ! -f "$DFLASH_DRAFT" ]; then
    warn "Draft path $DFLASH_DRAFT not found — running without draft"
    DRAFT_ARG=""
fi

[ "$GPU_COUNT" -gt 1 ] && warn "${GPU_COUNT} GPUs detected — native server layer sharding is not auto-enabled"

# ── build + exec native server ────────────────────────────────────────────
CMD=("$DFLASH_SERVER_BIN" "$DFLASH_TARGET"
     --host "$DFLASH_HOST"
     --port "$DFLASH_PORT"
     --max-ctx "$DFLASH_MAX_CTX"
     --prefix-cache-slots "$DFLASH_PREFIX_CACHE_SLOTS")

[ -n "$DRAFT_ARG" ]                && CMD+=(--draft "$DRAFT_ARG")
[ -n "$DRAFT_ARG" ]                && CMD+=(--ddtree --ddtree-budget "$DFLASH_BUDGET")
[ "$DFLASH_LAZY" = "1" ]           && CMD+=(--lazy-draft)
[ -n "$DFLASH_CACHE_TYPE_K" ]      && CMD+=(--cache-type-k "$DFLASH_CACHE_TYPE_K")
[ -n "$DFLASH_CACHE_TYPE_V" ]      && CMD+=(--cache-type-v "$DFLASH_CACHE_TYPE_V")

if [ "$DFLASH_PREFILL_MODE" != "off" ]; then
    [ -n "$DFLASH_PREFILL_DRAFTER" ] || die "DFLASH_PREFILL_MODE=$DFLASH_PREFILL_MODE requires DFLASH_PREFILL_DRAFTER"
    [ -f "$DFLASH_PREFILL_DRAFTER" ] || die "Prefill drafter not found at $DFLASH_PREFILL_DRAFTER"
    CMD+=(--prefill-compression "$DFLASH_PREFILL_MODE"
          --prefill-keep-ratio "$DFLASH_PREFILL_KEEP"
          --prefill-threshold "$DFLASH_PREFILL_THRESHOLD"
          --prefill-drafter "$DFLASH_PREFILL_DRAFTER")
fi

info "lucebox-hub container starting (target=$(basename "$DFLASH_TARGET"), max_ctx=$DFLASH_MAX_CTX, budget=$DFLASH_BUDGET, lazy=$DFLASH_LAZY)"

cd "$DFLASH_DIR"
exec "${CMD[@]}"

    int         prefix_cache_cap = 32;  // prefix cache slots (0 disables)

    // Thinking-budget v2 (mirrors antirez/ds4 ds4_eval.c knob names).
    // Applied when a request opts in via `thinking: {type: "enabled"}`.
    // think_max_tokens caps the phase-1 reasoning generation; the combined
    // (reasoning + content) cap is the request's max_tokens, defaulting to
    // default_max_tokens when omitted. The phase-1/phase-2 reprompt logic
    // itself is a follow-up PR — this PR ships only the wire surface
    // (CLI flag + finish_details emission) so consumers can probe + plan.
    int         think_max_tokens    = 10000;
    int         default_max_tokens  = 16000;

    // /props introspection inputs — captured at startup by server_main so
    // the /props handler doesn't need to crack open BackendArgs or env.
    // Matches dflash/scripts/server.py:1221-1312 field-for-field.

    std::string model_path;            // bargs.model_path
    std::string draft_path;            // bargs.draft_path (empty if no draft)
    std::string tokenizer_id;          // tokenizer name from GGUF metadata (best-effort)

    std::string kv_cache_v;            // effective KV V type

Traced the full chain. The `--think-max-tokens` is **never set** by lucebox's launch path:

1. `lucebox.sh serve` → `docker run` with `DFLASH_*` env vars (from `lucebox/docker_run.py:server_run_spec`)
2. The env list passed has 5–10 `DFLASH_*` knobs but **nothing think-related** (no `DFLASH_THINK_MAX`)
3. `dflash/scripts/entrypoint.sh` builds the `dflash_server` argv from those env vars — its CMD array has 9 conditional flags, none of which is `--think-max-tokens`
4. Server falls through to the hardcoded default in `dflash/src/server/http_server.h:59`:
   ```cpp
   int think_max_tokens   = 10000;
   int default_max_tokens = 16000;
   ```

### How our defaults compare to upstream antirez/ds4

| Knob | Upstream ds4_eval.c | Our dflash_server default |
|---|---|---|
| Combined `max_tokens` | 16000 | 16000 ✓ |
| `think_max_tokens` | **15488** (= 16000 − 512 reply reserve) | **10000** (hardcoded) |
| Implicit reply reserve | 512 tokens | 6000 tokens |

So when the bench sends `max_tokens=16000` with `thinking: enabled`:
- **Upstream**: thinking can use up to 15488 tokens, only 512 floor reserved for the visible reply
- **Ours**: thinking caps at 10000, the server reserves 6000 tokens for the reply

For ds4-eval that's a meaningful gap on hard reasoning:
- Easy COMPSEC/GPQA cases: thinking finishes well under 10000 → no difference
- AIME math + tough GPQA Diamond: reasoning can blow past 10000 → phase-2 reprompt forces an answer with truncated reasoning → wrong number. The May-21 partial trace's AIME garbage answers (case 51's `1000...000` with 60+ zeros) match exactly this failure mode
- Visible replies are short (a single letter or short integer) — 512 floor is plenty; 6000 reserve is wasted budget

Bragi's just-landed `3e8323d fix(cpp-server): phase-2 gate diagnostics + entrypoint draft-path + max_tokens banner` adds a startup banner for these values, so once we run the server they'll be visible in logs.

### Two options before the re-run

1. **Run as-is** — accept the 10000 cap; numbers will be improved by the 4096 → 16000 fix but still constrained on hardest cases
2. **Bridge the gap** — add `DFLASH_THINK_MAX` env support in `docker_run.py` + `entrypoint.sh`, default it to 15488 to match upstream. Small change, ~10 lines.

Want me to wire up option 2 before the re-run, or leave it to bragi since they're actively working in this area?
do 2. monitor for bragis changes and merge them

Doing both. First the THINK_MAX wiring, then setting up the bragi-watch loop.

206	def save(cfg: Config, path: Path | None = None) -> Path:
207	    path = path or default_config_path()
208	    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
209	    payload = _to_dict(cfg)
210	    # Write atomically: tomli_w → bytes via a tmp file → rename.
211	    tmp = path.with_suffix(".toml.tmp")
212	    tmp.write_bytes(tomli_w.dumps(payload).encode("utf-8"))
213	    tmp.replace(path)
214	    return path

> #!/usr/bin/env bash
> # Canonical launcher for the Luce DFlash OpenAI server on this rig.
> #
> # Config validated against live agent traffic on 2026-05-14. Each flag has
> # a reason; don't change one without re-running the corresponding sweep.
> #
> #   --max-ctx 98304
> #       Below the 120,320 capacity cliff documented in
> #       dflash/baselines/max_ctx_scaling_summary.md. Headroom for 64K+
> #       prompts. Going to 114688 was inside the safe envelope per the
> #       synthetic sweep but the live server hit roughly half the bench
> #       throughput, so we hold at 98K until that gap is understood.
> #
> #   --cache-type-k/v q4_0
> #       Explicit — server.py's default auto-selects tq3_0 at max_ctx>6144,
> #       which measured ~half the decode tok/s of q4_0 on real agent prompts.
> #       The dflash/baselines/ sweeps were also q4_0; staying on q4_0 keeps
> #       the deployed config consistent with the validated baseline.
> #
> #   --prefix-cache-slots 0
> #       Workaround. The inline-snapshot path hits an `empty_slot` abort
> #       under load (see /tmp/dflash_server_98304_q4.log). Disabling the
> #       cache trades the prefix-reuse speedup for reliability. Re-enable
> #       (default 1) once that path is fixed.
> #
> #   (no --lazy-draft)
> #       --lazy-draft adds ~1s of park/unpark to every request and was
> #       triggering client-side timeouts on streaming chat completions
> #       (46/50 requests aborted at 0 tokens during the first session).
> #       The full draft fits in VRAM at q4_0 KV + 98K ctx, so keep it warm.
> #
> #   --budget 22 / --fa-window 2048
> #       DDTree budget sweet spot and z-lab Qwen3.6 draft's SWA window.
> #
> # Logs:
> #   $DFLASH_SERVER_LOG  (default /tmp/dflash_server.log)
> #
> # To run in foreground (recommended for first launch — watch the readiness
> # line "Uvicorn running on http://0.0.0.0:1236"):

## Findings

### 1. Submodule `fattn.cu` (82 lines) — **flash-attention dispatch tracer**
Pure instrumentation: prints kernel choice, Q/K/V shapes/types/strides, and mask shape per FA call. Gated by env vars:
- `DFLASH27B_FA_TRACE=1` — enable
- `DFLASH27B_FA_TRACE_MAX` — line limit (default 200)

This is a **debugging tool for the decode-goal max-ctx investigation** (matches the May-14 GOAL doc). Not a fix — a probe. Not on any submodule branch, not on `bench-agent-loop-faithful-replay` (that branch's submodule fixes are unrelated cuMem/TQ3 work, and its tip is already merged into our current submodule HEAD).

**Recommendation**: Save it on a feature branch inside the submodule (`feat/fa-dispatch-trace`) so it's recoverable, then reset the submodule. Or discard if the investigation is dead.

### 2. Stash@{0} ≠ the untracked May-14 files
Important correction to my earlier read: `git stash` only captured **tracked-file modifications** at the time:
- `dflash/scripts/bench_llm.py` (+389/-20)
- `dflash/test/test_dflash.cpp` (+21/-20)

The untracked files (`start_server.sh`, `decode_goal_retry_loop.sh`, GOAL doc, `dflash/baselines/`) **were never in the stash**. They exist **only on disk here, in no other branch or stash**. If you delete them, they're gone.

The stash's bench_llm.py mods (+389 lines — substantial) look like real bench work that may or may not have been incorporated into the easel/clean bench suite. Worth a diff before dropping `stash@{0}`.

### 3. Local clean is *older* content, not unique content
- Diff: 7,371 insertions / 976 deletions across 165 files between local and easel clean.

The tip's submodule bump (`cuMem pool extension fix`) is already incorporated into our current submodule via easel PR #4 — *but* the prefix-cache bench scripts themselves are unique and don't appear in easel/clean's bench suite. Hardware: RTX 3090 Ti 24 GB.

=== GOAL doc working diagnosis section ===

`DFLASH27B_FA_TRACE=1` shows tree verify attention now uses a window-sized mask
after the first fix:

```text
mask=[2080,32]
```

But K/V views still expose capacity-sized per-head stride:

```text
K_stride_tokens=131072
V_stride_tokens=131072
```

This suggests the hot path still pays for, or is memory-layout-limited by, the
persistent KV cache layout `[head_dim, max_ctx_alloc, n_head_kv]` even when the
logical FA window is about 2K tokens.

Later packing experiments refined this diagnosis: an env-gated same-type
contiguous K/V pack reduced the FA trace stride to the active window
(`K_stride_tokens=2071`), but synthetic 4K at `max_ctx=131072` stayed flat to
slightly worse (`23.04` tok/s versus `23.61` tok/s baseline). The corresponding
agent-history check was invalidated by a concurrent GPU training process. So
capacity-sized K/V stride alone is not currently proven to be the fix.

Bisection found a sharp Q4 capacity cliff between `119808` and `120320`.
`119808` stays near fitted-context decode throughput for synthetic 4K,
synthetic 32K, and agent-history 4K prompts. `120320` collapses to roughly
one-third of fitted throughput with unchanged prefix correctness and acceptance
length.

## Non-Goals

- Do not optimize PFlash compression quality or NIAH scoring in this goal.
- Do not change draft-model training or acceptance heuristics unless required
  to preserve correctness.
- Do not run multiple resident target copies to work around the issue.
- Do not regress fitted-context decode throughput to improve oversized-context
  throughput.

## Acceptance Criteria

Primary performance gate:

- Agent-history prompt, `prompt_tokens=4096`, `max_ctx=131072` reaches at least
  `0.75x` of fitted-context decode tok/s with identical generated-token prefix.
  Current post-mask-fix result is `4.61 / 23.42 = 0.20x`.

Secondary performance gates:

I have clear verdicts now. Let me lay them out and act on the unambiguous one.

### May-14 files — **useful, save them**

The work was a real perf investigation:
- **GOAL doc**: bisected a sharp Q4 KV decode cliff between `max_ctx=119808` and `120320`; agent-history 4K prompt at max_ctx=131072 dropped to `0.20x` of fitted-context decode tok/s.
- **`dflash/baselines/`**: ~20 measured-on-RTX-3090-Ti JSONs + summary that back the doc's claims. Reusable as historical reference.
- **`decode_goal_retry_loop.sh`**: generic overnight-benchmark orchestrator (locking, GPU-busy detection, iteration state). Reusable beyond decode-goal.
- **`start_server.sh`**: dflash_server launcher with each flag's rationale documented from the sweeps. Lower-level than `lucebox.sh`/lucebox CLI.

Easel/clean **directionally addressed the same problem** with different fixes:
- `c4a7ba6 gemma4: implement G5 SWA ring-buffer, G6 fa_window, G3 compress`
- `570a71f server: add --fa-window CLI flag to dflash_server`
- `2ee7462 fix(scripts): auto-fit --max-ctx to prompt size`
- `c1bd053 fix(server): sync max_ctx between HTTP server and backend KV cache`

[tool result] === bench-agent-loop: is the repro_empty_response.py useful, or covered elsewhere? ===
--- Header of the repro script ---
"""Standalone repro for the empty-response correctness regression in
dflash/scripts/server_tools.py + dflash/scripts/prefix_cache.py.

Sends a SEQUENCE of multi-turn chat completions to one warm dflash server.
Each call extends the conversation by one full
  assistant(tool_call) → tool(result) → user(continue)
turn, so the prefix grows naturally and the cache populates inline
snapshots between calls — the same pattern that
dflash/scripts/bench_agent_loop.py walks through transcripts.

Then runs the SAME sequence against a slots=0 server (cache disabled)
as the control.

The tool result is generated programmatically (synthetic pylint-like
output, deterministic per turn index) so this script has no dependency
on any private session transcript.

Expected: every call produces non-empty content. Observed against
PR #59: starting at some call N (often N=3 around 12-15K char prefix),
warm responses become content_len=0 / reasoning_len=0 / completion_tokens=0,
finish_reason="stop". The first broken call still does the prefill (slow
wall time) but emits nothing; subsequent calls hit the cache and return
empty in <50 ms.

Usage:
    python3 repro_empty_response.py \\
        --target /path/to/Qwen3.6-27B*.gguf \\
        --draft  /path/to/qwen3.6-27b-dflash \\
        --bin    /path/to/dflash/build/test_dflash \\
        --server /path/to/dflash/scripts/server_tools.py
"""
import argparse
import json
import signal
import subprocess
import sys
import time
import urllib.error
import urllib.request

### Running on other GPUs (4090, 5090, DGX Spark / GB10, Jetson AGX Thor)

Supported out of the box; the build just needs the right CUDA toolkit. `dflash/CMakeLists.txt` already auto-adds Blackwell archs when your nvcc is new enough, so the main quickstart above works as-is on newer cards.

| GPU | Arch | Min CUDA | Status |
|-----|:----:|:--------:|--------|
| Tesla P40 Pascal | `sm_61` | 12.0 | supported with scalar F16 fallback; needs 24 GB for the 27B stack |
| Tesla V100 Volta | `sm_70` | 12.0 | supported with F16 WMMA kernels |
| RTX 3090 Ampere | `sm_86` | 12.0 | **reference, all numbers above** |
| RTX 2080 Ti Turing | `sm_75` | 12.0 | supported, 53 tok/s DFlash verified (FP16 draft) |
| RTX 4090 Ada | `sm_89` | 12.0 | should work, unverified, pass `-DCMAKE_CUDA_ARCHITECTURES=89` |
| RTX 5090 Blackwell consumer | `sm_120` | 12.8 | **205 tok/s DFlash, 4.84× vs AR** (Q4_K_M, budget=40) |
| DGX Spark / GB10 | `sm_121` (compute capability 12.1) | 12.9 | supported, auto-added by CMake |
| Jetson AGX Thor | `sm_110` | 13.0 | supported, auto-added by CMake |

Verify your target:
```bash
python -c "import torch; p=torch.cuda.get_device_properties(0); print(p.name, 'sm_%d%d'%(p.major,p.minor), p.multi_processor_count,'SMs', round(p.total_memory/1e9,1),'GB')"
nvcc --version
```

**DGX Spark / GB10 quick start:**
```bash
# CUDA 12.9+ required for sm_121
nvcc --version  # must show >= 12.9
git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/Luce-Org/lucebox-hub && cd lucebox-hub/dflash
cmake -B build -S . -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release   # CMake auto-adds sm_121
cmake --build build --target test_dflash -j
```

**Jetson AGX Thor quick start:**
```bash
# CUDA 13.0+ required for sm_110 / AGX Thor.
nvcc --version
git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/Luce-Org/lucebox-hub && cd lucebox-hub/dflash
cmake -B build -S . -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release   # CMake auto-adds the Thor arch your nvcc supports
cmake --build build --target test_dflash -j
```

**Retune per GPU:**
- **DDTree `budget=22`** tuned for 3090 + Q4_K_M + 24 GB. On the RTX 5090, budget=40 is optimal (swept). On GB10 (128 GB unified), re-sweep — larger tree = more verify throughput until memory bandwidth saturates. `scripts/bench_llm.py --budget N` has the sweep hooks.
- **TQ3_0 KV cache + sliding `target_feat` ring** was shaped by 24 GB (fits up to 256K context on a 3090). On GB10 (128 GB unified) / 5090 (32 GB) you can push context further or skip quantization entirely and keep F16 KV.
- **Perf numbers** (207 tok/s demo, 129.5 HumanEval, 2.8× vs SGLang AWQ) are RTX 3090 @ stock. RTX 5090 numbers (205 tok/s HumanEval, 4.84×) are in [RESULTS.md](dflash/RESULTS.md). Ada/GB10/Thor not yet swept, PRs with `RESULTS.md` entries welcome.

### Running on other GPUs (4090, 5090, GB10 / DGX Spark)

Supported out of the box; the build just needs the right CUDA toolkit. `dflash/CMakeLists.txt` already auto-adds Blackwell archs when your nvcc is new enough, so the main quickstart above works as-is on newer cards.

| GPU | Arch | Min CUDA | Status |
|-----|:----:|:--------:|--------|
| RTX 3090 Ampere | `sm_86` | 12.0 | **reference, all numbers above** |
| RTX 4090 Ada | `sm_89` | 12.0 | should work, unverified, pass `-DCMAKE_CUDA_ARCHITECTURES=89` |
| RTX 5090 Blackwell consumer | `sm_120` | 12.8 | supported, auto-added by CMake |
| GB10 / DGX Spark, Jetson Thor | `sm_121` | 12.9 | supported, auto-added by CMake |

Verify your target:
```bash
python -c "import torch; p=torch.cuda.get_device_properties(0); print(p.name, 'sm_%d%d'%(p.major,p.minor), p.multi_processor_count,'SMs', round(p.total_memory/1e9,1),'GB')"
nvcc --version
```

./test_dflash target.gguf draft.safetensors prompt.bin 128 out.bin \
  --draft-gpu=0 --target-gpus=0,1 --target-layer-split=1,3 \
  --target-split-load-draft

./test_dflash target.gguf draft.gguf prompt.bin 128 out.bin \
  --draft-gpu=0 --target-gpus=0,1 --target-layer-split=1,1 \
  --target-split-dflash
```

When more than one target GPU is listed, `test_dflash` runs a non-daemon
target-only layer-split harness. Each target GPU loads only its assigned
contiguous layer range; `output_norm` and `output.weight` stay on the last
target GPU. `--target-split-load-draft` additionally loads the DFlash draft
on `--draft-gpu` (`.safetensors` or quantized draft `.gguf`), captures target
features into a draft-side mirror, and runs a small draft forward smoke. This
allows capacity checks where the draft and a target layer range share one GPU
before serving integration. `--target-split-dflash` runs the same split target
placement through a chain DFlash decode loop and reports acceptance length.

**Python flags on `scripts/run.py`, `scripts/server.py`:**
```bash
python3 scripts/run.py --ctk q8_0 --ctv q4_0 --prompt "hello"
python3 scripts/run.py --cache-type-k q8_0 --cache-type-v q4_0 --prompt "hello"
```

**TQ3 semantics under asymmetry:** TQ3_0 is K-side-driven. The FWHT rotation is applied to the query and inverse-applied to the attention output only when `K=TQ3_0`. V's type is independent of rotation. The 256-stride context alignment is triggered if *either* K or V is TQ3_0.

Legacy `--kv-tq3` / `--kv-q4` / `--kv-f16` flags continue to work as symmetric shorthand for backward compatibility.

## Qwen3.6-27B Target

Qwen3.6-27B is the default integration path. It uses the same `qwen35` target architecture as Qwen3.5, and the docs use the Lucebox GGUF DFlash draft by default.

```bash
# 1. target
hf download unsloth/Qwen3.6-27B-GGUF Qwen3.6-27B-Q4_K_M.gguf --local-dir models/

# 2. matched 3.6 draft (GGUF, used by default by scripts/run.py and server.py)
hf download Lucebox/Qwen3.6-27B-DFlash-GGUF dflash-draft-3.6-q8_0.gguf --local-dir models/draft/

# 3. bench
DFLASH_TARGET=models/Qwen3.6-27B-Q4_K_M.gguf python3 scripts/bench_he.py --n-gen 128
```

The default draft path is discovered under `models/draft/`. Scripts prefer `dflash-draft-*.gguf`, then any `.gguf`, then `model.safetensors`. Explicit `.gguf` and safetensors drafts still work via `DFLASH_DRAFT` / `--draft`; qwen35-compatible targets remain swappable via `DFLASH_TARGET` / `--target`.

## Native C++ HTTP server

`dflash_server` serves the same client-facing local API surface used by the
harnesses without the Python `scripts/server.py` wrapper. It supports `/health`,
`/v1/models`, OpenAI Chat Completions including streaming and tool metadata,

# Luce DFlash benchmark results

Single RTX 3090 24 GB, CUDA 12, driver 535.
Target: `unsloth/Qwen3.5-27B-GGUF` (Q4_K_M, ~16 GB).
Draft:  `z-lab/Qwen3.5-27B-DFlash` (BF16, 3.46 GB).
Concurrency = 1, greedy decoding, `n_gen=256`.
Reproduce with `python3 scripts/bench_llm.py` (samples 10 prompts/dataset, seed=42).

## Headline — AR vs Luce DFlash at concurrency 1

| Task      | AR tok/s | DFlash tok/s | AL   | Speedup |
|-----------|:--------:|:------------:|:----:|:-------:|
| HumanEval | 37.78    | **129.52**   | 8.31 | **3.43×** |
| Math500   | 37.71    | **110.51**   | 7.04 | **2.93×** |
| GSM8K     | 37.65    | **96.15**    | 6.14 | **2.55×** |

AR = autoregressive target-only decode via `test_generate`.
DFlash = block-diffusion draft + DDTree budget 22 verify + fast rollback.
AL = mean committed tokens per draft/verify step (acceptance length).

Datasets pulled live via HuggingFace `datasets`:
- HumanEval — `openai_humaneval`, `prompt` field
- GSM8K    — `gsm8k` main split, `Question: … Answer: ` format
- Math500  — `HuggingFaceH4/MATH-500`, `Problem: … Solution: ` format

11-  * `DS4_EVAL_MAX_TOKENS` mirrors ds4_eval.c's `max_tokens` default
12:    (16000) — the combined cap covering reasoning + reply. The thinking
13-    budget split is server-side configuration (`--think-max-tokens`),
14-    not wire protocol, so cross-server comparisons stay clean.
